content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable Rose Marie Sheetz 
Councilmember 
City of Morro Bay 
595 Harbor Street 
Morro Bay, CA 
93442 
Dear Councilmember Sheetz: 
May 2, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. G-88-152 
We are in receipt of your request for advice regarding 
Mayor Reddell's duties under the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act"),!! and the 
city attorney's obligations to disclose information regarding 
possible conflicts of interests of members of the council. 
In your letter you refer to the Commission's Skousen Advice 
Letter, No. A-87-062 (copy enclosed), in which we advised that 
Mayor Reddell was prohibited from participating in decisions 
which involved restrictions on water connections because of the 
foreseeable material financial effect on his business and real 
property interests. 
Based on this letter you now ask whether 
Mayor Reddell has a continuing conflict of interest requiring 
that he refrain from participation in any discussion which 
would affect a change in the method of allocating water, or 
discussions which concern pursuing and obtaining new water 
sources for the city. 
section 83114 authorizes the Commission to respond to 
requests for written advice with respect to the requestor's 
duties under the Act. 
Regulation 18329(b) (8) provides that 
requests for formal written advice may be declined where: 
(A) 
The requestor is seeking advice relating to 
past conduct. 
!! Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


Honorable Rose Marie Sheetz 
May 2, 1988 
Page 2 
(B) 
The requestor is seeking advice about 
another person's duties under the Act and has not been 
authorized to do so by that person. 
(C) 
It appears that material facts provided in 
the request may be inaccurate, incomplete, or in 
dispute. 
(D) 
The request does not present a question 
under the Political Reform Act .... 
It appears that your request for advice has not been 
authorized by Mayor Reddell and that it may involve past 
conduct. 
Moreover, you have not provided sufficient facts on 
which we could base an analysis of the current situation. 
Finally, your question regarding the city attorney's 
obligation to disclose information appears to involve 
attorney-client privilege, which the Act does not address. 
This type of information differs from advice provided by the 
commission, where the advice request and the written advice are 
public records. 
(Regulation 18329(b) (6).) 
You will note that 
I have enclosed the Skousen Advice Letter, supra, for your 
information, and would be happy to forward any other advice 
letters you feel would be helpful to you. 
Nevertheless, if you 
wish to pursue this question, I suggest you contact the State 
Bar of California. 
Due to these circumstances, your request for advice is 
declined pursuant to Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A), (B), (C), and 
(D) • 
DMG:LS:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
G31~co~nsef 
By: 
Lill 
~~ 
Coun el, Legal Division 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Rose Marie Sheetz 
Councilmember 
city of Morro Bay 
595 Harbor Street 
Morro Bay, CA 
93442 
Dear Ms. Sheetz: 
April 25, 1988 
Re: 
88-152 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on April 22, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Lilly spitz, an attorney in the 
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
• 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
withiri 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • 
(9 I 6) 322,,)6AO

sections.question_synthetic: Does Mayor Reddell have an ongoing conflict of interest that prohibits him from participating in discussions about water allocation methods and new water sources, and what disclosure obligations does the city attorney have regarding council members' conflicts of interest?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the request lacked authorization from Mayor Reddell, may involve past conduct, lacks sufficient factual support, and includes a question about attorney-client privilege outside the scope of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is a declination letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document includes preliminary acknowledgment letter (April 25, 1988) and main declination letter (May 2, 1988). The request was declined on four regulatory grounds. No affirmative advice was provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Mayor Reddell has a continuing conflict of interest requiring that he refrain from participation in any discussion which would affect a change in the method of allocating water, or discussions which concern pursuing and obtaining new water sources for the city, and what are the city attorney's obligations to disclose information regarding possible conflicts of interests of members of the council.

CONCLUSION: Due to these circumstances, your request for advice is declined pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), (B), (C), and (D). It appears that the request for advice has not been authorized by Mayor Reddell and that it may involve past conduct. Moreover, the requestor has not provided sufficient facts on which an analysis of the current situation could be based. Finally, the question regarding the city attorney's obligation to disclose information appears to involve attorney-client privilege, which the Act does not address.

In your letter you refer to the Commission's Skousen Advice Letter, No. A-87-062 (copy enclosed), in which we advised that Mayor Reddell was prohibited from participating in decisions which involved restrictions on water connections because of the foreseeable material financial effect on his business and real property interests. Based on this letter you now ask whether Mayor Reddell has a continuing conflict of interest requiring that he refrain from participation in any discussion which would affect a change in the method of allocating water, or discussions which concern pursuing and obtaining new water sources for the city. section 83114 authorizes the Commission to respond to requests for written advice with respect to the requestor's duties under the Act. Regulation 18329(b) (8) provides that requests for formal written advice may be declined where: (A) The requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct. !! Government Code sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 Honorable Rose Marie Sheetz May 2, 1988 Page 2 (B) The requestor is seeking advice about another person's duties under the Act and has not been authorized to do so by that person. (C) It appears that material facts provided in the request may be inaccurate, incomplete, or in dispute. (D) The request does not present a question under the Political Reform Act .... It appears that your request for advice has not been authorized by Mayor Reddell and that it may involve past conduct. Moreover, you have not provided sufficient facts on which we could base an analysis of the current situation. Finally, your question regarding the city attorney's obligation to disclose information appears to involve attorney-client privilege, which the Act does not address. This type of information differs from advice provided by the commission, where the advice request and the written advice are public records. (Regulation 18329(b) (6).) You will note that I have enclosed the Skousen Advice Letter, supra, for your information, and would be happy to forward any other advice letters you feel would be helpful to you. Nevertheless, if you wish to pursue this question, I suggest you contact the State Bar of California. Due to these circumstances, your request for advice is declined pursuant to Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A), (B), (C), and (D) •
embedding.summary: On May 2, 1988, the FPPC declined Councilmember Sheetz's request for advice regarding Mayor Reddell's conflict of interest in water-related decisions and the city attorney's disclosure obligations, citing lack of authorization, insufficient facts, and issues beyond the scope of the Act. The letter referenced a prior FPPC advice letter (Skousen A-87-062) that had found Mayor Reddell had a conflict regarding water connection restrictions.
